title: Chelyocarpus wallisii
taxonomy: Chelyocarpus wallisii is classified within the family Arecaceae, commonly known as the palm family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its palmate leaves and solitary inflorescence. The genus Chelyocarpus is a group of palm species native to Central and South America. Chelyocarpus wallisii specifically is a species of palm endemic to Ecuador. Its classification as a member of the Arecaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other palms.

intro: Chelyocarpus wallisii, also known as the Wallis' palm, is a species of palm tree native to South America, characterized by its robust trunk and feathery leaves. The Wallis' palm has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms and promote recovery. The bioactive constituents of Chelyocarpus wallisii include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, the Wallis' palm is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
